title: Three teams that make perfect sense for Browns' QB Shedeur Sanders
description: Here are three teams that make perfect sense for Cleveland Browns' QB Shedeur Sanders in a potential trade this offseason.
fullText:
The Cleveland Browns have once again found themselves in the midst of a quarterback battle leading up to the 2026 NFL season.

This offseason, general manager Andrew Berry did not make any significant moves to overhaul the quarterback room for next season, with 2026 sixth-round pick Taylen Green being the lone addition to the room. This leaves the Browns with veteran Deshaun Watson and 2025 fifth-round pick Shedeur Sanders as the two names that could be in line for the starting job in 2026, with the battle taking place throughout the course of the offseason.

However, in a scenario where Watson is able to beat out Sanders for the QB1 spot, there’s a good chance that Berry and the front office could move off the 24-year-old playmaker before the season to recoup additional picks for the 2027 NFL Draft. The rumors of a potential Sanders trade have already begun to gain momentum this offseason, as ESPN Cleveland's Tony Rizzo revealed on Monday that teams have been checking in on his availability.

"There are talks and ongoing calls about the availability of Shedeur Sanders," - Rizz's antenna is UP about the Browns QB situation right now 👀👀👀 pic.twitter.com/T6XyD4jffl

— ESPN Cleveland (@ESPNCleveland) June 22, 2026

Here are three teams that make perfect sense as landing spots for Sanders.

Could another former Browns’ starting quarterback make his way to Tampa Bay this offseason?

While the Buccaneers found great success with Baker Mayfield at the helm in his first two seasons with the organization, his decline in production last year could open the door for the front office to search for a replacement. And with Mayfield entering the final year of his contract, Tampa Bay is the perfect destination for Sanders to spend the 2026 season behind the 31-year-old veteran and compete for the starting job in 2027.

Similar to the Buccaneers’ quarterback situation with Mayfield, the Detroit Lions will always be in the conversation for a young apprentice behind Jared Goff.

While the former No. 1 pick in the 2016 NFL Draft signed a massive extension with the organization back in 2024, the Lions could save roughly $30 million in cap space if the team cut him after Jun 1st in 2027, according to Over The Cap. Depending on how well Goff performs during the upcoming season, Sanders could be the young quarterback to take over for Goff in the near future.

The Packers are known for developing backup quarterbacks into potential starters, with former 2022 third-round pick Malik Willis the most recent player in the pipeline in 2025.

Now, with Willis joining former defensive coordinator Jeff Hafley in Miami for the 2026 season, Green Bay could roster another backup quarterback behind starter Jordan Love. Willis was able to appear in four games for the Packers last season, which was enough to make him a hot commodity in free agency. Landing with a well-oiled team like the Packers would be a perfect spot for Sanders to develop behind Love, while also increasing his value in 2027 and beyond.

This article originally appeared on Browns Wire: Three teams that make perfect sense for Browns' QB Shedeur Sanders

title: One Browns reporter makes blunt statement about QB Deshaun Watson
description: One Browns reporter isn't holding back about Watson's play in offseason practices.
fullText:
The Cleveland Browns are set to enter another training camp with questions around the quarterback position, with a competition between Deshaun Watson and Shedeur Sanders. Cleveland has Dillon Gabriel and rookie Taylen Green on the roster as well, but they aren't likely to snag the starting job unless there's an injury or two.

It's been up and down and not promising this spring with both quarterbacks struggling at times, especially when it comes to Deshaun Watson. Rumors have swirled for weeks about who might have a leg up in the race to be the Browns starter, but one Browns reporter didn't hold back on what he's seen from Watson this year.

Zac Jackson from The Athletic had this to say about Watson during an interview on 92.3 The Fan;

"I think it's a fair Spring conclusion to say he is the same player he's been here. Inconsistent player, not accurate, bad decisions, he's just not any good... I hope they're coming around to that thought."

"I think it's a fair Spring conclusion to say he is the same player he's been here. Inconsistent player, not accurate, bad decisions, he's just not any good... I hope they're coming around to that thought."

fullText:
Detroit Lions fans can officially start counting down the days until training camp.

According to Dave Birkett, Lions rookies will report to training camp on July 25, while veterans are scheduled to arrive on July 28.

title: Will Seahawks WR JSN break Calvin Johnson’s REC yards record in 2026?
description: Seahawks star Jaxon Smith-Njigba was close to breaking the NFL’s single-season receiving yards record in 2025 and has the potential to do so in 2026.
fullText:
Seattle Seahawks star wide receiver Jaxon Smith-Njigba was not far from breaking Former Detroit Lions great Calvin Johnson’s single-season receiving yards record in 2025, and may have the potential to do so next season.

Johnson stated earlier in June that Los Angeles Rams star WR Puka Nacua has the best chance out of all current players in the NFL to break his receiving yards record, which stands at 1,964 yards from the 2012 season. Nacua has the talent to do so, having recorded 1,715 yards in 2025.

The same applies for Smith-Njigba as well. The reigning AP Offensive Player of the Year was the only receiver to go for more yards than Nacua last season, with 1,793.

JSN is arguably the best deep-ball threat in the league. The Seahawks will likely lean on their passing attack slightly more to begin next season, with questions surrounding their offensive backfield, which may open the door for Smith-Njigba to receive more targets on the year.

With his efficiency, as well as his ability to rack up yards after the catch, even more looks thrown his way has the potential to breed about the most prolific season that the NFL has ever seen from a wide receiver in 2026.

This article originally appeared on Seahawks Wire: Seahawks vs Lions: can JSN break Calvin Johnson's record in 2026?

title: Wimbledon champion Marketa Vondrousova handed four-year suspension for refusing drugs test
description: 2023 Wimbledon champion offered ‘no compelling justification’ for her refusal, says ITIA
fullText:
2023 Wimbledon champion Marketa Vondrousova has been suspended for four years for refusing a drug test.

Vondrousova, the former world No 6, refused an out-of-competition test by a doping control official on 3 December 2025 and was subsequently charged by the International Tennis Integrity Agency (ITIA), which oversees tennis’ anti-doping policy, for failing to provide a sample in April this year.

The ITIA announced on Monday that an independent tribunal ruled Vondrousova’s evidence during the hearing offered “no compelling justification” for her refusal of the test.

Vondrousova released a statement this April saying that she had “reached a ⁠breaking point after months of physical and mental stress” at the time of the random visit and suffered an “acute stress reaction” which had prevented her from thinking rationally.

Tennis players are required to log their whereabouts for an allotted hour each day with anti-doping authorities so they can be tested outside of competition, although tests can also occur outside these set windows.

The officer who visited her in December arrived at 8.15pm, outside her whereabouts submission slot for the day, and demanded an immediate test, which Vondrousova described as a “serious intrusion into my privacy”.

The ITIA said on Monday: “Under anti-doping rules, the starting point for a sanction when a player refuses a test must be the same as if they had tested positive. This is to ensure that anyone who is doping cannot serve a shorter ban simply by refusing to be tested.”

The ITIA’s chief executive Karen Moorhouse said: “We understand that the testing process is uncomfortable, and acknowledge that it is an additional burden for players whose jobs already come with a high level of pressure and scrutiny, but it is essential to protect fair competition.

“Safety and welfare of players and our testers is really important to us. Our testers are well-trained, professional, and the gender of our testing witness always matches the player. They carry ID at all times, and players are able to verify their identity in other ways if they are ever unsure.”

Vondrousova explained during the hearing that stress and poor mental health affected her decision-making, as well as concerns for her safety, and said that the tester failed to properly identify themselves.

The tribunal considered her explanations as well as evidence from the doping control officer who had attempted to conduct the test, and determined there was “no compelling justification” for her refusal.

The full written decision will be released in “due course”, the ITIA added.

Moorhouse said: “Unpredictable testing is an essential tool to protect clean sport.

“The independent tribunal ultimately supported that principle. This case is an important reminder that players can be tested at any time, in any place, and that refusal comes with significant risk.”

In her statement in April, released on Instagram, Vondrousova cited the case of double Wimbledon champion and her compatriot Petra Kvitova, who was brutally attacked by an intruder at her home in 2016, suffering severe stab wounds to her left playing hand.

Vondrousova said: “After what happened to ⁠Petra, we don’t take strangers at our door lightly.”

She continued: “The recent doping control incident happened because I reached a breaking point after months of physical and mental stress.

“Years of hateful messages and threats have affected how safe I feel in my own space. When someone rang my door ‌late at night without properly identifying themselves or ‌following protocol — I reacted as a person who felt scared.

“In that moment, it was about feeling safe, not about avoiding anything.”

She revealed that experts confirmed she suffered an Acute Stress Reaction and Generalised Anxiety Disorder, adding: “In that moment, fear clouded my judgment and I just couldn’t process the situation rationally.”

Now ranked 122nd in the world, Vondrousova last played on the WTA Tour in Adelaide this January.

The 26-year-old has the right to appeal to the Court of Arbitration for Sport, as do the ITIA and the Czech national federation. She not be allowed to play in, coach at, or attend any events organised or sanctioned by any of tennis’ four major bodies: the ITF, ATP, WTA, or the grand slams, as well as any organised by any national association, during her four-year ban.

Her suspension will end on 21 June 2030. She had originally been slated to appear at Wimbledon next week.

title: Missouri football lands highly ranked OL in 2027 recruiting class
description: Missouri football added a highly ranked in-state offensive lineman to its growing 2027 recruiting class on Monday.
fullText:
Missouri football’s active recent run on the recruiting trail continued Monday morning, when the Tigers added a highly ranked offensive lineman to their 2027 class.

Four-star offensive lineman Kyler Kuhn announced Monday via social media that he has verbally committed to Mizzou’s Class of 2027. Kuhn is a Kansas City native who plays his high school football at St. Pius X.

Kuhn is the third player to commit to Mizzou in the past two days, taking the Tigers’ 2027 class to 17 total verbal commitments.

“All glory to God in the Highest!” Kuhn posted to his official X account Monday. “Wouldn’t be possible without all the support from my family, coaches and teammates! Grateful for this opportunity.”

All glory to God in the Highest! Wouldn’t be possible without all the support from my family, Coaches, and teammates! Grateful for this opportunity! Thank you @Hayesfawcett3 for the Graphic! #MIZ https://t.co/8nD45KsA7I

— Kyler Kuhn (@KylerKuhn) June 22, 2026

Kuhn is listed at 6-foot-3 and 280 pounds and likely will be an interior offensive lineman at the collegiate level. He has won back-to-back MSHSAA Class 3 wrestling titles at 285 pounds.

The offensive lineman visited Mizzou in May. He also took visits to Iowa, Nebraska and Penn State in recent weeks.

More: What Missouri football should expect from new era at Arkansas in 2026

More: What's changed at KU ahead of Missouri football's Border War visit

In the recruiting service 247Sports’ composite rankings, Kuhn is ranked as the No. 221 player nationally and the No. 10 interior offensive lineman in his class. He is rated the No. 5 player in Missouri in this cycle, meaning Mizzou has secured commitments from three of the top five players within the state.

Kuhn is the third player in the past two days to commit to head coach Eli Drinkwitz’s next class. He joins three-star cornerback T’ari Miller out of Miami and three-star offensive tackle Jaylen Hill, who each announced their pledges to Mizzou on Sunday, June 21.

With 17 commitments now on board, Mizzou now is expected to have the No. 27-ranked class in the country in 247Sports’ ratings. That will take the Tigers to No. 9 in the SEC when the rankings update.

The early national signing period for football players in the Class of 2027 is in December.

This article originally appeared on Columbia Daily Tribune: Missouri football lands OL Kyler Kuhn to Class of 2027 recruiting

title: Ty Simpson: I appreciate the big brother role Matthew Stafford's playing in my development
description: When the Rams selected quarterback Ty Simpson to be Matthew Stafford's successor in April, it was a surprise to many.
fullText:
When the Rams selected quarterback Ty Simpson to be Matthew Stafford's successor in April, it was a surprise to many.

But Simpson was well aware of Los Angeles' interest in him, even if he denied that publicly until well after the night he was drafted.

Now as Simpson settles in, he's leaning on the man he'll eventually replace to further his understanding of his new scheme. Stafford has said Simpson hasn't been shy about asking questions, which is a positive given the nature of the position.

"I hope I’m not annoying him,” Simpson said recently, via Gary Klein of the L.A. Times, “but I really appreciate the big brother kind of role he’s playing in my development.”

Simpson added that Stafford is “the MVP for a reason.”

“Everything he does, he’s so just aware of everything, aware of how everything works,” Simpson said. “I really appreciate him and Stetson taking me in and answering all the questions I have.”

The No. 13 overall pick of this year’s draft, Simpson is likely to get extensive reps during preseason games as he continues to get his feet wet in the league. The Rams traditionally have not played starters in preseason games under head coach Sean McVay.

title: 🚨 Official: Sergio Canales returns to Racing Santander
description: Sergio Canales officially returns to what was once his home: Racing de Santander.The Cantabrian midfielder returns to the club he called home, from the youth academy all the way to his move to Real Ma...
fullText:
Sergio Canales officially returns to what was once his home: Racing de Santander.

The Cantabrian midfielder returns to the club he called home, from the youth academy all the way to his move to Real Madrid in 2010.

With the team from El Sardinero back in the First Division, Canales is also returning to La Liga, which he left in 2023.

After his spell with Rayados de Monterrey, where his contract expires this coming June 30, he is coming home.

Without a doubt, this is one of the most exciting signings of the upcoming 2026/2027 season.

title: Illinois edges past TCU to add commitment from 2027 WR Brody Knowles: ‘They’ve been trending in the right direction’
description: After navigating a rigid official visit schedule, Brody Knowles has locked in his decision. The playmaker from Southlake (Texas) Carroll committed to Illinois on Monday, days after wrapping up his official visit to Champagne. The Fighting Illini edged past TCU and Arkansas for the three-star WR, who loves his fit within Bret Bielema’s program. “They […]
fullText:
After navigating a rigid official visit schedule, Brody Knowles has locked in his decision.

The playmaker from Southlake (Texas) Carroll committed to Illinois on Monday, days after wrapping up his official visit to Champagne.

The Fighting Illini edged past TCU and Arkansas for the three-star WR, who loves his fit within Bret Bielema’s program.

“They have an amazing program there and over the last couple of years, they’ve really been trending in the right direction,” Knowles told Rivals after returning from his official visit.

“Coach (Justin) Stepp, Coach (Barry) Lunney, and Coach B are such great people, and they always showed me so much love,” Knowles said. “Really showed me the family vibe they have up there. Stepp’s development and record are basically like no other, and my ultimate goal is to play in the NFL.”

Stepp has been recruiting the Dallas Metroplex pass-catcher for years and has had Illinois right in the thick of this recruitment throughout the spring.

TCU was also a factor with Knowles, who enjoyed his time at Arkansas earlier this month.

Illinois adds a vertical threat who amassed 816 yards receiving with seven touchdowns as a junior last fall. Knowles averaged north of 22 yards per catch.

“Coach B and Lunney have also put a lot of players in the league, too. The race was super tight, and I loved all three schools. Great coaches and people at all three of them,” he said.

title: Autzen Stadium among top 10 toughest places to play in EA Sports CFB 27
description: Autzen Stadium is known for holding a rowdy Oregon fanbase, earning a spot among the ten toughest places to play in EA Sports College Football 27.
fullText:
Few venues around the nation are feared by teams as much as Autzen Stadium. The Oregon Ducks are known for packing the stadium and making enough noise to impact the game on Saturdays, earning a reputation as one of the loudest and most difficult places to play in the country.

The upcoming EA Sports College Football 27 video game ranked the top 25 toughest places to play around the country, and the Ducks found their home venue near the top. Autzen Stadium checked in at No. 6 on the list, up three spots from last year's edition of the video game.

The Ducks seldom lose when playing in Eugene. Despite only holding 60,000 fans, the Oregon crowd makes a difference in big games, throwing the opposing offense off its game and slowing down the game operations. Oregon held the nation's longest winning streak at home at 18 before losing to the eventual national champion Indiana Hoosiers in October.

Still, Autzen Stadium wasn't the highest-ranking Big Ten venue on the list. Ohio State's Ohio Stadium ranked second on the list, while Penn State's Beaver Stadium checked in at No. 3. The Ducks rode into the "White Out" at Penn State last season and escaped with a double overtime victory, and will look to do the same this season when they travel to face the Buckeyes in November.

🏟️ The Toughest Places to Play in #CFB27 are here.

Surely everyone will agree with this list. pic.twitter.com/XuVl6pu2it

— College Football 27 (@EASPORTSCollege) June 22, 2026

It is a bit of a shock not to see Autzen Stadium ranked as the toughest Big Ten venue, given the noise and impact from Oregon's crowd compared to Ohio State and having defeated Penn State on the road last season.

LSU's Tiger Stadium took the top spot on the list, holding over 100,000 people. Georgia and Alabama ranked fourth and fifth, respectively, just ahead of the Ducks. Florida, Tennessee, Clemson and Texas A&M's home venues rounded out the top 10.

Washington's Husky Stadium ranked No. 15 and Iowa's Kinnick Stadium ranked No. 19 on the list. Oregon secured victories at both stadiums last season. Among the rest of the Big Ten, Michigan Stadium, the largest venue by capacity in the country, ranked No. 12, and Indiana's Memorial Stadium placed No. 17.

Autzen Stadium will get to show off its game-changing impact this fall with several big games coming to Eugene. Oregon will host Boise State to kick off the season, and later host UCLA, Michigan and Washington in pivotal conference games.

title: Training camp report dates, joint practice schedule released for Jaguars
description: The NFL has released the training camp report dates for the Jaguars along with their joint practice schedule.
fullText:
Training camp report dates and the joint practice schedule for the Jacksonville Jaguars have been announced by the NFL.

The Jaguars' rookies will report for training camp on July 25 and the veterans on July 28.

As far as joint practices go, we already knew that the Jaguars would hold three joint practices this summer, one before each preseason game. Now we know the dates.

Training camp report dates and joint practices are officially set. 

The Cardinals and Panthers veterans report one month from today. pic.twitter.com/zfrvwnSNlB

— Tom Pelissero (@TomPelissero) June 22, 2026

Below is a look at the Jaguars' preseason schedule:

Aug. 15: at New Orleans, 4:00 PM ET (FOX30)

Aug. 21: vs. Carolina, 7:30 PM ET (CBS47)

Aug. 28: vs. Tampa Bay, 7:30 PM ET (FOX30)

Last year, the Jaguars had only one joint practice. With three on this year's schedule, that gives Liam Coen the flexibility to not play his starters in the preseason, with those players getting good practice time against another team's ones.

Joint practices provide an opportunity for players, particularly starters and key contributors, to get some game-like reps against an opponent, while still being in a somewhat controlled practice environment.

title: Dan Hurley believes Alex Karaban is a Round 1 NBA draft pick
description: The UConn coach believes his former player is a surefire first rounder in the NBA draft
fullText:
UConn head coach Dan Hurley is not Alex Karaban's agent heading into the 2026 NBA draft, but the former Husky is being advertised as a worthy first round pick by his former coach.

Hurley has talked up Karaban's intangibles in recent days, which he believes any team in the NBA could use.

“You see how important situational basketball is when you watch the Finals, getting a guy like Alex on your team who plays great situational basketball is so critical," Hurley told the Hartford Courant.

It is an interesting comment for Hurley to make, considering teams that are picking in the latter stages of the first round of the draft potentially have a great chance to make the NBA Finals next season.

Dan Hurley believes UConn’s NBA Draft hopefuls Alex Karaban and Tarris Reed Jr. should each be strongly considered as first round picks. https://t.co/9If5hajhEz

— UConn Insider (@UConn_Insider) June 20, 2026

Hurley believes that Karaban can be an "All-NBA role player", and doesn't think there's much of a decision when it comes to taking a seasoned college basketball veteran over an unknown.

“I mean, if I’m a team in the 20s and I’ve got a choice between maybe a moderately more talented player that has questionable makeup in terms of building a championship or a winning organization, just how do you pass on Karaban in the 20s," Hurley said.

USA TODAY Sports' most recent mock draft has Karaban outside of the first round, with former UConn teammate Tarris Reed Jr. being selected with the final pick in the first round.

title: Giannis Antetokounmpo trade rumors: Celtics reportedly going all-in, have 'real shot' to land two-time MVP
description: This trade would be a gamble for the Celtics, moving away from something they know works to the risk of Antetokounmpo's health.
fullText:
As recently as last week, league sources talking to NBC Sports were playing down the idea of Boston as a serious suitor for Giannis Antetokounmpo, saying they weren't really all-in on their pursuit.

That appears to have changed over the weekend. First, NBA insider Marc Stein of the Stein Line wrote in a late-night missive Sunday:

"One league source, after this story was published, told The Stein Line that the Celtics managed to emerge from the weekend 'with a real shot' to win this race with a Jaylen Brown-centric offer, adding that Milwaukee has considered going ahead with a Boston swap even without a third-team facilitator."

Monday morning, ESPN’s Brian Windhorst said on Get Up, "I was really skeptical that the Boston Celtics were going to go all-in on this, but from what I understand, they have."

The expectation remains that this trade will be completed before Tuesday night's NBA Draft. Miami is still near the front of the line and may yet win this sweepstakes with an offer reportedly involving Tyler Herro, Kel'el Ware, Jamie Jaquez Jr. and draft picks. However, it's clear Milwaukee doesn't love that offer, or this trade would already be done.

ESPN's Shams Charania added this on the network’s Get Up show: "It's going to consist of Milwaukee and likely one of these two teams. There's no third team, fourth team in any construct right now."

Boston's offer would be based around All-NBA wing Jaylen Brown, coming off the best season of his career, averaging 28.7 points, 6.9 rebounds and 5.1 assists, leading the Celtics to 56 wins and the No. 2 seed in the East despite Jayson Tatum being out for most of the season. The talk in league circles had been that Boston didn't firmly put Brown in an offer, that Milwaukee had been using the situation more as leverage to get more out of Miami, but that appears not to have changed.

What other players (if any) and draft compensation would be in a Boston trade are the real questions. There have been rumors about Payton Pritchard being in the offer, but he would be a player the Celtics would want to keep because of how well he would fit playing off Antetokounmpo. Stein suggests it would be a young player, such as Hugo Gonzalez, plus draft picks. Boston has the No. 27 pick in Tuesday's NBA Draft, which likely would be part of the package.

Would Milwaukee keep Brown and try to build around him, or flip him to another team in a second trade? The argument for a second trade is that the Bucks are rebuilding and a 27-year-old player in his prime does not fit that timeline. Does Milwaukee believe that what it could get in return from Boston, plus a second Brown trade, outweighs what Miami has on the table?

Or, does the new lottery system, which punishes the teams with the three worst records in the league, change the calculus for Milwaukee, which now does not want to go into rebuild mode? Windhorst reported Brown is open to the idea of having "his own team," suggesting he is open to the idea.

Trading for Antetokounmpo would be a huge gamble for the Celtics.

We know that Brown and Tatum work together — they have won a title as a lead duo. If Boston spends this offseason filling out the roster around them — specifically upgrading along the front line — it enters next season as one of the true contenders to come out of the East, along with New York, Detroit and a healthy Indiana.

Antetokounmpo, at his best, is a two-time MVP and a better player than Brown, giving the team an elite dowhill attacker. Celtics president Brad Stevens talked in his postseason press conference about the need for the team to put more pressure on the rim, and nobody does that better than Antetokounmpo. He also is a two-way force. The risks are that he is also older (31) and has a growing injury history that has sidelined him or slowed him in each of the last three playoffs. On top of that, he will demand a new max contract after the trade, and it's likely that as he ages, that will be an anchor in the last season or two.

That risk is countered by the vision of just how good Boston could be with Antetokounmpo and Tatum. (Plus, Antetokounmpo becomes valuable looking ahead to a potential matchup with Victor Wembanyama and the Spurs, not many people have the size, strength and athleticism to bother Wemby.)

If this trade falls apart and Antetokounmpo heads to South Beach, it leads to another issue: Has Boston crossed the Rubicon with Brown and now has to trade him? Has it gone so far down that road that the relationship has soured and the two sides need to part ways? There will be a lot of speculation about that, but it's a question only Brown and Stevens can really answer, and both are intelligent men who can at least talk it out. If they choose to.

title: NFL announces 4 key dates for San Francisco 49ers preseason
description: We finally know when the San Francisco 49ers will report for training camp and hold joint practices with the Chargers and Titans.
fullText:
The San Francisco 49ers are less than a month out from rookies reporting for training camp according to an announcement by the NFL on June 22.

San Francisco's rookies will report for training camp on July 18, with veterans reporting a week later on July 25. Practice dates haven't been announced, but those typically begin the day after veterans report.

The NFL also dished out joint practice dates across the league. The reported joint practices for the 49ers with the Tennessee Titans and Los Angeles Chargers are now official.

San Francisco will host Tennessee for a joint practice at their practice facility on August 11. Those teams will face off in their preseason opener two days later on August 13.

Then the 49ers will head to El Segundo, Calif. the next week for a joint practice with the Chargers on August 18. Their preseason bout is two days later on August 20. There will be no joint practice for the 49ers with the Las Vegas Raiders before their preseason finale on August 27.

Exact dates for 49ers practices will be announced by the team at a later date.

More 49ers: Past and present 49ers QBs throwing at TE University this week

This article originally appeared on Niners Wire: 49ers 2026 training camp start date, joint practice dates announced

title: Longtime SportsCenter anchor retiring
description: ESPN has seen many great anchors come and go over the years. Now, another legend and staple of the network’s flagship program, SportsCenter, is joining the group of people who have departed the network. According to Awful Announcing, Linda Cohn will be leaving the Worldwide Leader after her contract runs out at the end of…
fullText:
ESPN has seen many great anchors come and go over the years. Now, another legend and staple of the network’s flagship program, SportsCenter, is joining the group of people who have departed the network.

According to Awful Announcing, Linda Cohn will be leaving the Worldwide Leader after her contract runs out at the end of the month, as the sides failed to agree on a new role for her moving forward.

Cohn had enjoyed the work-life balance of her role hosting the 1 AM Eastern SportCenter that was produced from Los Angeles and relocated to the West Coast for the job. However, ESPN decided to move 1 AM SportsCenter back to Bristol in May of 2025, resulting in Cohn’s role shifting to hosting SportsCenter from Bristol and other special projects.

After 34 years at ESPN, cross-country flights and hotel stays became too much of an obstacle to continuing her career at the network. An official announcement “is expected as early as tomorrow, with multiple editions of SportsCenter having Cohn to celebrate her time with the company planned for later in the week,” per Awful Announcing.

Many of ESPN’s stalwarts who have departed the network, a list that includes Kenny Mayne, Neil Everett, and Stan Verett, have gone on to launch personal projects. Evertett and Verett host a twice-a-week Twitch show. Cohn already teased her next career chapter earlier this month in a plug for her YouTube channel.

New Chapter. Join me. Creating content often. Let’s face it, I have a lot to say.

— Linda Cohn (@lindacohn) June 1, 2026

Regardless of what’s next, 34 years in the business is as great a run as they come.

The post Longtime SportsCenter anchor retiring appeared first on The Comeback: Today’s Top Sports Stories & Reactions.

title: Kyle Kuzma's Cryptic Posts Could Point To Giannis Antetokounmpo Trade Being Done Amid Celtics Rumors
description: The Boston Celtics are reportedly one of the two finalists for Giannis Antetokounmpo in a trade with the Milwaukee Bucks, and Kyle Kuzma could be hinting at a deal being done.
fullText:
Kyle Kuzma's Cryptic Posts Could Point To Giannis Antetokounmpo Trade Being Done Amid Celtics Rumors originally appeared on NESN. 

The Boston Celtics are reportedly one of the two finalists for Giannis Antetokounmpo in a trade with the Milwaukee Bucks, and Kyle Kuzma could be hinting at a deal being done.

On Monday morning, NBA insider Shams Charania reported that Antetokounmpo will be traded before Tuesday's NBA Draft and that the Celtics have a real chance to land him. It's unclear exactly what the Celtics can offer, outside of Jaylen Brown. It is also unclear whether anyone else would be headed to Boston with Antetokounmpo.

Well, Kyle Kuzma may be hinting at the fact that a deal is done and fans will soon find out. NBA insider Bill Simmons reported on Sunday that Kuzma could be included in a trade as a "Giannis tax."

He posted eight bomb emojis, hinting at a bombshell report incoming, before saying a "major announcement" was coming "soon." To top things off, he replied to a post about him being included in a potential Antetokounmpo trade.

— kuz (@kylekuzma) June 22, 2026

Major announcement soon 🙏🏽👊🏽💪🏽

— kuz (@kylekuzma) June 22, 2026

"Since when is a 6’9 wing who plays any role you ask, guards 1–5, shot almost 50% from the field and cares about defense a ‘throw‑in’? lol," Kuzma said in response to Simmons' comments.

Kuzma is an NBA champion and averaged 13 points last season. He is a solid player, but not a superstar. However, he could play a role on any team that acquires Antetokounmpo if he's included.

More NBA: When Is NBA Draft 2026? Schedule, Channel, Live Stream & Full List of Celtics Picks

title: Warriors 'still interested' in drafting recent NCAA champion
description: According to NBA insider Jonathan Wasserman, the Warriors are 'still interested' in selecting Yaxel Lendeborg with the No. 11 pick.
fullText:
Although lots of speculation has surrounded the Warriors and their potential interest in Iowa point guard Bennett Stirtz, the team still may target a forward with the No. 11 overall selection instead. According to Jonathan Wasserman of Bleacher Report, sources say that Golden State is "very interested" in selecting him at No. 11.

While Lendeborg may not have the highest ceiling considering his age (23) as opposed to other prospects, there's no denying what he could bring to the Warriors rotation right away. Last season at Michigan, Lendeborg led the Wolverines to an NCAA title, averaging 15.1 points, 6.8 rebounds, 3.2 assists, 1.2 blocks and 1.1 steals. Listed at 6'9" with a 7'3" wingspan, Lendeborg's defense and decision-making could quickly make him a mainstay in Golden State's rotation.

This article originally appeared on Warriors Wire: Golden State 'still interested' in drafting recent NCAA champion

title: Eagles 2026 training camp: When will rookies and veterans report
description: The Eagles’ rookies and veterans will report to the Jefferson Health Training Complex on July 28, with a joint practice against the Patriots scheduled for Aug. 19.
fullText:
The Philadelphia Eagles now have another key date on the calendar as the countdown to training camp continues.

Rookies and veterans are scheduled to report on July 28 to the Jefferson Health Training Complex, giving Philadelphia its official starting point for one of the most important summers of the Nick Sirianni era. The Eagles enter camp with a reshaped roster, several important position battles, and major offensive questions following the A.J. Brown trade, making the opening of camp more than a routine checkpoint.

The July 28 report date will bring the full roster together as Philadelphia begins building toward the 2026 regular season. Jalen Hurts will work through a new offensive structure under Sean Mannion, DeVonta Smith will step into a larger role as the clear No. 1 receiver, and newcomers such as Makai Lemon, Hollywood Brown, Dontayvion Wicks, and Elijah Moore will try to establish timing in a revamped wide receiver room. The offensive line will also be under focus as Lane Johnson, Cam Jurgens, and Landon Dickerson work back from injury-related concerns while Tyler Steen attempts to build on his momentum.

The Eagles’ defense will have its own major storylines under Vic Fangio, including the continued development of young core pieces such as Jalen Carter, Quinyon Mitchell, Cooper DeJean, Nolan Smith, and Andrew Mukuba. Training camp will help determine how the linebacker rotation settles, how the secondary aligns, and which depth players can force their way onto the 53-man roster.

Philadelphia will also have a notable joint practice on Aug. 19 against the New England Patriots. Joint practices often provide a better evaluation than preseason games because starters can receive controlled, high-intensity reps against a different opponent. For the Eagles, the Patriots' practice will offer a useful benchmark late in camp, particularly for the revamped passing game, offensive line communication, and the defensive backfield.

The Eagles still have plenty to sort out before Week 1, but the framework is set. The full roster reports July 28, and the Aug. 19 joint practice with New England will give Philadelphia one of its most important tests of the summer.

This article originally appeared on Eagles Wire: Eagles announce training camp report date, Patriots joint practice

title: Nationals .791 OPS breakout former Rays prospect bust could be 'best trade bait'
description: This infielder might be a sneaky trade candidate this season.
fullText:
Nationals .791 OPS breakout former Rays prospect bust could be 'best trade bait' originally appeared on The Sporting News. Add The Sporting News as a Preferred Source by clicking here.

The Washington Nationals are not going to be sellers this season if they keep playing the way they have this season. At 40-38, the Nationals are just 0.5 game out of a postseason spot.

They've got to this point thanks to an incredible offense led by CJ Abrams and James Wood, two players likely to be All-Stars this season. But, they're not the only players having good seasons.

Former Tampa Bay Rays prospect bust Curtis Mead is playing great for the Nationals this season with a strong .791 OPS. But, Jim Bowden of The Athletic named Mead as an "under-the-radar" trade candidate who might be their "best trade bait" if they fall out of postseason contention.

"The Nats are currently in position to make a postseason run, so the only way they'll trade Mead is if they fall out of the race before Aug. 3," Bowden writes. "However, if they do, he could be their best trade bait."

While the Nationals might not have any plan to trade away players this summer as of now, a rough end to June and poor July might open the door to some moves.

There's bound to be plenty of attention on Wood and Abrams, who likely won't be traded. Mead is one player whom the Nationals should consider moving.

Mead is 25 years old, and from 2022 through 2025, he posted -0.2 bWAR with a .238 batting average and a .617 OPS in 152 games and 445 at-bats.

MORE: Mets have eight projected trade chips, but only half, including Freddy Peralta, might be sought-after

He was clearly not a good player, but now, with a .791 OPS and 121 OPS+, he's broken out into a good hitter who could be an intriguing trade target.

Bowden named Mead as an "under-the-radar" trade target, and someone who might be the Nationals' best trade bait, for good reason.

If there is a team willing to trade for Mead, who's having a breakout season and is under club control through the 2030 season, then the Nationals could likely get a solid return.

Washington might not believe Mead's breakout season is for real, and if that's the case, then trading him this season, only if they falter from now until the trade deadline, might not be a bad idea at all.

Phillies reportedly turn down Bryce Harper's outfield offer to help at trade deadline

Giants are ‘hopeful ‘ they can trade Matt Chapman by MLB deadline

Angles can demand

title: Cooper Nicholson reflects on UNC’s Game 2 win vs. Oklahoma
description: UNC’s Cooper Nicholson hit a pivotal two-run homer in Game 2 vs. Oklahoma. In this postgame Q&A, he breaks down the at-bat and the Tar Heels’ mindset.
fullText:
North Carolina kept its championship hopes alive with a 6-2 win over Oklahoma in Game 2 of the College World Series final on Sunday, forcing a decisive Game 3 on Monday night. The Tar Heels got dominant pitching from starter Caden Glauber and key production throughout the lineup to even the best-of-three series.

Glauber delivered five shutout innings, allowing just one hit and striking out eight to quiet the Sooners’ bats. At the plate, first baseman Erik Paulsen went 3-for-5, while third baseman Cooper Nicholson provided a pivotal two-run home run in the seventh inning to extend North Carolina’s lead.

The winner-take-all Game 3 is scheduled for Monday at 7 p.m. ET on ESPN. After Sunday’s win, head coach Scott Forbes, along with Glauber, Nicholson and Paulsen, met with reporters to discuss the performance and look ahead to the championship showdown.

Here’s what Nicholson had to say.

Coach Forbes gave me the light on 3-0. And I swung at a bad pitch. I should not have swung at that. I was just sticking through the at-bat, looking for something over the play. Got a breaking ball and swung at it.

I think we’re a tough group offensively. We put together some gritty at-bats, as you saw. We got on base a ton. French, he went, I think, 0-for-0 with four walks. That’s just showing what our offense is like.

Follow us @TarHeelsWire on X and like our page on Facebook to follow ongoing coverage of North Carolina Tar Heels news, notes and opinions.

This article originally appeared on Tar Heels Wire: UNC Baseball: Cooper Nicholson after UNC win over Oklahoma

title: Iran thanks host Los Angeles, calls for peace in World Cup note
description: Players from the Iranian men's soccer team left a note in their locker room to thank World Cup host Los Angeles and call for peace among nations.
fullText:
June 22 (UPI) -- Players from the Iranian men's soccer team left a note in their locker room to thank World Cup host Los Angeles and call for peace among nations.

The handwritten letter was placed inside their locker room after a scoreless draw with No. 10 Belgium on Sunday at Los Angeles Stadium. The No. 22 Iranians secured their first point of the tournament through a 2-2 draw with No. 84 New Zealand on June 15 on the same field in Inglewood, Calif.

"From the ancient Persia of thousands of years ago to the civilized Iran of today, the spirit of Iran remains alive and steadfast," the note said.

"We came to Los Angeles with pride, competed with honor, and leave with dignity. Thank you, Los Angeles, for your hospitality. And thank you to every Iranian who gave their heart, voice and soul for Iran throughout these 180 minutes. May peace, respect and friendship prevail among all nations."

The Iranians' presence at the World Cup has been surrounded by tension amid the country's war with Israel and the United States -- one of three World Cup host nations. U.S. and Iranian officials remain active in negotiations to end the conflict.

Several Iranian team staff members were denied entry visas into the United States for the tournament. The Iranians also switched their base camp from Arizona to Mexico because of strict travel restrictions. Their visa conditions stipulate that they can fly into the United States the day before a game, but they must leave the same day as the match.

Iranian players donned pins, which said "168," on their clothes when they arrived to their base camp in Tijuana. The number signifies the reported death toll from a bombing of an elementary school in Minab, Iran on the first day of the war. The Iranians also wrote "168" and "Minab" on their locker room letter.

The school was adjacent to buildings used by Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps Navy -- a paramilitary force designed as a terrorist organization by the United States and more than a dozen other countries.

U.S. officials told The New York Times, Washington Post and NBC that the school was hit due to an intelligence error. Those outlets, which said the United States was likely responsible for the bombing, reported that the school was previously part of the base.

The bombing was part of a wave of strikes in Iran, which led to the death of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

Department of Homeland Security chief Markwayne Mullin told Fox News on Sunday that a man connected to the IRGC, who was posing as the president of Iran's soccer federation, was among several people prevented from entering the United States on Saturday when the team was leaving Tijuana.

Mullin said most teams travel with around 120 people and Iran was permitted to travel to the United States with 53 people. He said those denied for travel "had direct ties to the IRGC and aren't their normal traveling group."

Last week, coach Amir Ghalenoei called Iran "the most oppressed" team at the World Cup due to travel issues and other restrictions. Captain Mehdi Taremi called the situation a "disaster" when meeting with reporters last week.

Some fans could be heard booing during Iran's national anthem before each of their first two games at the World Cup. Iran will face Egypt in a Group G finale at 11 p.m. EDT Friday in Seattle.

No. 26 Egypt (1-0-1) currently sits atop Group G, with four points. Iran (0-0-2) sits in second, just ahead of third-place Belgium (0-0-2). New Zealand (0-1-1) rounds out the group.

Iran could still face the No. 13 Americans in the World Cup's knockout stage. The winner of Group G will face a third-place team from Group A, E, H,I or J in the Round of 32. The winner of that match will face the Americans or a third-place team from Group B, E, F, I or J in the round of 16.

If the Iranians finish second or third in Group G, they wouldn't face the Americans until the World Cup final if the teams progress that far.

title: Fans react to UM's Dusty May departure for Dallas on social media
description: There was mixed reaction to Monday's news.
fullText:
Fan reaction to the news that University of Michigan men's basketball coach Dusty May is leaving for a job in the NBA came fast and furious on social media Monday.

"Can't ever get mad when a college coach goes to the pros to give it a shot," Barstool Sports founder — and noted Michigan alum — David Portnoy said in a video posted on his Facebook page and on X.

"I was going to name a horse Dusty Gray," he said, wearing a UM baseball cap and Team USA soccer jersey. "I will no longer be doing that."

He went on to wish the departing coach the best of luck.

"He brought us a championship," Portnoy said. "It sucks for Michigan. Not the Monday news I wanted to hear."

Basketball fan Nerderous applauded the news.

"One of the brightest minds in basketball," he said on X. "Player development. Modern basketball. Winning culture."

But UM fan Jeff Riger panned the move on X.

"I’ll always love Dusty May for bringing Michigan Hoops a National Championship," he wrote. "But at the same time … Screw you Dusty! Brutal news."

This article originally appeared on The Detroit News: Fans react to UM's Dusty May departure for Dallas on social media

title: NBA Draft 2026: Don't make the Brunson second-gen mistake again. Draft Cameron Boozer No. 1
description: The top prospect debate between Cameron Boozer, AJ Dybantsa and Darryn Peterson shouldn't be a debate at all.
fullText:
As the championship glow continues to surround Jalen Brunson, it's worth reminding ourselves about a certain prospect's résumé.

Son of a longtime NBA player. Grew up immersed in the NBA lifestyle. Won big at every level, including high school and at one of America's top college programs. Earned the Wooden award, Naismith award, AP Player of the Year award, Sporting News Player of the Year award. Left early to declare for the NBA draft.

Despite all these credentials, this player was not considered the favorite to go No. 1 in the draft. Concerns about his underwhelming athleticism and stocky size held him back.

Oh, you thought I was talking about Brunson?

I'm talking about Cameron Boozer, the Duke prospect who should be drafted No. 1.

It's true that the Knicks' Finals MVP checks off all of the above boxes, and famously fell all the way to the second round of the 2018 draft before being taken 33rd by the Dallas Mavericks. Boozer isn't in danger of sliding out of the top 5, much less the first round, but it appears teams are making the same mistake of overlooking Boozer's impact.

BYU standout AJ Dybantsa and Kansas star Darryn Peterson are considered far more likely to be drafted No. 1, while Boozer is a longshot to hear his name called first on Tuesday — even though Boozer is younger, has won more, has posted stronger numbers and has NBA pedigree.

NBA Mock Draft 10.0: Latest projections for every pick

It's that last thing — being the son of an NBA player — that should be seen as a superpower of genetics and knowledge, but instead may be a subconscious dent in his NBA stock.

An 18-year-old Boozer, who compiled a season in which he averaged more points than Peterson (a pure scorer), more 3-pointers and assists than Dybantsa (a skilled wing), and nearly more rebounds than those two players combined, should go No. 1.

Add in Boozer's NBA pedigree, and the top prospect debate shouldn't be a debate at all.

How did everyone overlook Brunson? It's not just the story of the NBA Finals; it's the story of his basketball career.

Despite dominating the Chicago high school circuit and having the name recognition of being the son of Rick Brunson, who played nine seasons in the NBA, Jalen was not listed among the top 15 in any major recruiting poll in his class. Still, he continued to dominate at the collegiate level, winning two championships at Villanova and sweeping the National Player of the Year awards by his junior year.

But Brunson was snubbed again on draft night. After being recognized as the unanimous top player in college, he watched Villanova teammate Mikal Bridges go No. 10. Then another teammate, Donte DiVincenzo, go No. 17. Then another, Omari Spellman, who averaged 10 points per game for Villanova, go No. 30. It wasn't until the 33rd pick in the draft that Brunson heard his name called.

The pattern continued in the NBA, where he backed up Luka Dončić in Dallas for the majority of his first four years. It wasn't until he joined the Knicks in 2022 that Brunson's career finally took off. Three All-NBA appearances later, Brunson averaged an NBA-high 28.4 points per game this postseason, while performing like Michael Jordan in the clutch en route to a title. Brunson doesn't have many doubters now.

The fact that Brunson dominated at every level but still couldn't get the respect he deserved from the eyes of basketball cognoscenti proves his NBA pedigree didn't outweigh his smaller stature. He was hampered with questions about his athleticism and defensive viability. On the Roommates Show podcast, former Mavericks owner Mark Cuban shared the scouting report on Brunson before they picked him at No. 33.

"A little chubby, not quite as fast, not super athletic, but he's a winner," Cuban said.

The same questions about athleticism surround Cam, limiting his perceived upside compared to his peers. Having an NBA player as a father would seem like a feather in the cap for draft prospects, but it may backfire in subtle ways. The word "potential" drives so much of draft evaluation, and prospects like Dybantsa and Peterson don't have NBA fathers who could artificially cap their ceiling at the next level. (No. 1 pick Andrew Wiggins, whose father Mitchell played six years in the league, was an exception thanks to his jumping ability. )

Rick Brunson was a journeyman. Boozer made "only" two All-Star teams. For Dybantsa and Peterson, it's a blank slate. We tend to think of them as having more upside perhaps because we don't subconsciously have equivalent images of Rick's or Carlos' careers in our heads when we peg Dybantsa's and Peterson's projections.

For Cam Boozer, he's facing a different battle — a cognitive bias that he, like Jalen before him, must overcome.

Two years ago, when Bronny James was hoping to be selected in the NBA draft, I wrote a deep dive on the phenomenon of the NBA bloodline and pointed out that Bronny, being the son of LeBron James, had a good shot of sticking around in the NBA. I stood by that even when he was selected 55th overall, a spot in the draft when most players in that range don't amount to much of anything in the big show, if at all.

The Bronny selection generated accusations of basketball nepotism, but the historical data suggests that the opposite may be the case: the sons of NBA fathers are unfairly judged on draft day.

My optimism in Bronny's case was buoyed by the track record of legacy NBA players like Brunson, and I still think he'll carve out a solid NBA career with his improved shooting. In 2024, sons of NBA players had outperformed their draft slot by a whopping 30%, which was higher than any major college program, including NBA talent factories like Kentucky (+26%), UCLA (+24%) and Michigan State (+17%).

Since that story ran, the second-gen effect has continued to show its lasting power, though the overall improvement is now 26% above expectations. Brunson didn't just become an All-Star again — he leveled up and became a champion and Finals MVP, joining Stephen Curry and Kobe Bryant as the third son of an NBA player to win that award since 2010.

Curry, 38, is still averaging 26.6 points per game. The 40-year-old Al Horford (son of Tito) and 36-year-old Klay Thompson (son of Mychal) remain key rotation players at a time when most NBA players their age are shaving strokes off their golf game.

The younger NBA "sons" are doing just fine. At 21 years old, Reed Sheppard, son of Kentucky legend Jeff Sheppard, who made a brief stint in the league, was among the top vote-getters for Sixth Man of the Year and Most Improved Player of the Year. Second-round picks Nic Claxton (son of Charles), Trayce-Jackson Davis (Dale) and Jabari Walker (Samaki) continue to punch above their weight.

Beyond that, the story of the NBA Finals was not just Brunson's ascension, but also the emergence of San Antonio Spurs rookie Dylan Harper. Turning just 20 years old on March 2, Harper became the Spurs' go-to scorer at the guard position, averaging 18 points, 6.4 rebounds and 3 assists per game. He showed strength and feel well beyond his years, scoring more Finals points than any age-20 player since Magic Johnson in 1980, according to Stathead tracking.

Maybe we shouldn't have been too surprised by Harper's marvelous play. After all, he is the son of 15-year vet and five-time NBA champion Ron Harper.

Drafted second in the 2025 draft, Harper is already making some experts wonder whether he should have gone No. 1 overall over Cooper Flagg, who won Rookie of the Year. If history holds, we could be looking at the same conversation for Cam Boozer this time next year.

Jalen Brunson. Kobe Bryant. Stephen Curry. Al Horford. Devin Booker. Kevin Love. Klay Thompson. Domantas Sabonis. Darius Garland. Jaren Jackson Jr. Wally Szczerbiak. Andrew Wiggins.

These are the 12 All-Stars who were sons of NBA players and selected in the NBA draft since the modern era that began in 1989. If that sounds like a lot, that's because it is.

Of the 2,155 players drafted since 1989, 9% of them went on to become All-Stars. But if your dad played in the NBA? That percentage nearly doubles to 16.4%. Players who have NBA fathers being nearly twice as likely to become All-Stars is partly due to the fact that some of them were drafted high and therefore somewhat expected to reach all-league status. Wiggins was No. 1 overall and Horford was No. 3 overall. Is making an All-Star that big of an accomplishment?

But then there's Brunson at No. 33 who has made three All-Star squads. Bryant at No. 13 made 18 All-Star teams. Booker, drafted 13th overall in 2015, missed this year's All-Star festivities, but has five to his name. Same with Thompson (11th overall in 2011) and Sabonis (drafted 11th in 2016). When we account for draft slot, we'd only expect nine All-Star players out of the pool of 73 draftees of second-generation prospects. Boozer will attempt to be the 13th on the list.

Something's going on here. I've argued that second-generation players fight a reputation that they've been handed a silver spoon and therefore don't have the same drive as other prospects. But as we saw with Brunson and Harper in the Finals, the opposite can be the case. When other players wilt under the bright lights, Brunson and Harper seem unmoved by the pressure.

In his postgame press conference, Brunson said he didn't feel overwhelmed by the moment and even pointed to his father's experience bouncing around the league as an example of true pressure.

"No pressure, no pressure whatsoever," Brunson said. "My dad being on eight or nine unguaranteed contracts throughout his career and not knowing when you're going to get cut, when a team is going to move on from you, while your family is on the East Coast and you are wherever you are in the country. That's pressure.

"Working out three times a day in the summertime and watching him push himself just to get a training camp deal, that's pressure. I'm very fortunate to be in the position I am and I definitely think I worked pretty hard. So when the opportunity presented itself like it did (in Game 5), I just trusted my work. … I'm just never afraid to fail."

During the Finals, Harper said he picked his father's brain about performing on the biggest stage as a rookie. Ron Harper's message?

"Be you," Dylan Harper said his father told him. "You don't have to switch who you are."

As a matter of identity, Harper and Brunson are members of an exclusive club of the NBA bloodline, something that should give Boozer a clear edge on draft night.

But as my colleague Kevin O'Connor points out in his Draft Guide, Boozer faces questions that might keep him from going No. 1: "The worry is that he's not a vertical athlete and the foundation of all that production — overpowering smaller defenders — gets diminished against NBA length."

Again, the concerns sound eerily similar to Brunson, who blasted through every slight he heard leading up to the draft and throughout his career (Becky Hammon was far from the only one who questioned how his size would fare at the highest level). O'Connor has kept Boozer at No. 1 on his big board, but has predicted in his latest mock draft that Boozer will go lower on Tuesday.

To me, Boozer is the power forward version of Luka Dončić and Nikola Jokić — basketball savants who don't need sky-high verticals to dominate the game. Washington and Utah shouldn't think twice about drafting him.

If Boozer falls to Memphis at No. 3, there's a good chance it would be a mistake, not unlike when second-gen Curry fell to No. 7 due to similar size concerns. A team like the Oklahoma City Thunder, who are armed with the 12th and 17th pick, would be wise to try to move up and target Boozer, who brings the playmaking and power that they lacked in the San Antonio series. Chicago, which holds the No. 4 pick, should also attempt to jump up and bring Boozer, whose father starred for the Bulls for four seasons, to the Windy City.

With Brunson sitting atop the basketball world, one would think we have learned our lesson of respecting the second-gen star. But time and time again, legacy prospects like Brunson and Boozer are not taken as seriously as their credentials suggest. Is having an NBA father considered a feature or a bug on the résumé? If Boozer falls to No. 3, we have our answer.

title: 'Sachin's career almost got cut short': Ex-cricketer's chilling warning for Vaibhav Sooryavanshi
description: Former South Africa batter Daryll Cullinan expresses concern over the physical toll on 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's explosive batting style. Cullinan fears for the youngster's wrists and joints, citing the increased demands of modern cricket and heavier bats. He draws parallels to Sachin Tendulkar's career-threatening elbow injury, urging attention to Sooryavanshi's long-term health as his workload grows.
fullText:
Sooryavanshi has quickly earned a reputation for his fearless approach and remarkable power hitting. His ability to generate exceptional bat speed has become one of the defining features of his game. However, Cullinan believes that such an aggressive method could eventually take a toll on the youngster's body, especially as he is still developing physically.

“I’m in awe of this young man, but what's bothering me, and no one is talking about it, is injuries? At 15, swinging a big bat, playing so much cricket, I fear for his wrists, elbows, the smaller stuff and joints. What does medical opinion say? Remember, Sachin's career almost got cut short by a bad elbow. He definitely would not have been swinging so hard so often as Sooryavanshi,” Cullinan posted on LinkedIn after Sooryavanshi’s match-winning 94-run knock in the final of the Tri-Nation series in Dambulla.

Cullinan remains convinced that the stress generated by Sooryavanshi's powerful swings could place considerable pressure on his body over time.

“I think he would be on a fairly strict routine. It's the fact that he is still growing, and so too the joints, ligaments, muscles, I would presume? They will be taking strain, I can assure you,” Cullinan wrote, replying to one of the comments.

The former Proteas batter pointed to the example of Sachin Tendulkar, a player with whom Sooryavanshi is frequently compared. Tendulkar's celebrated career was threatened by a serious tennis elbow injury in 2004, an issue he later described as one of the most difficult setbacks he faced despite enduring numerous physical blows during his 24-year international journey.

Cullinan believes the modern game may present even greater challenges. With players using heavier bats and competing in a far busier cricket calendar than previous generations, he feels young cricketers face increased risks of long-term wear and tear.

“I know of a good few guys who picked up chronic wrist problems. The thing is, we were using lighter bats, playing far less cricket. He is still growing, which needs to be remembered. Today's training and recovery methods will help, but I do fear for his long-term future and health,” Cullinan, who played 70 Tests and 138 ODIs for the Proteas, pointed out.

131909089

title: A major college basketball shake-up and its effect on Kentucky
description: On Monday, the college basketball world experienced a huge coaching change that could have an effect on the Kentucky Wildcats.
fullText:
On Monday morning, the college basketball world experienced a seismic shake-up with the news that Michigan Wolverines head coach Dusty May was leaving to take the same position with the NBA's Dallas Mavericks. It's a shocking move that will certainly have an effect on the 2026-27 season, and potentially a direct effect on the Kentucky Wildcats.

Obviously, who Michigan hires as a replacement will be key. They will need to move quickly, as they try to keep their roster intact. Players will have a 15-day window where they can leave the program and go elsewhere that opens five days after they hire a coach, or automatically if they don't hire a coach within 30 days.

So how does the change affect Kentucky?

Most immediately, it improves the chances of the Wildcats landing Nikola Kusturica. Most analysts consider the UCLA Bruins and the Wolverines as Kentucky's top competitors for the Serbian wing. May's leaving could take Michigan out of the running.

In addition, if any of Michigan's current roster does decide to leave, the Wildcats have an open roster spot. A 2026 recruit like Brandon McCoy would have many suitors, and Mark Pope was in on him before he committed to Michigan.

The next step will be waiting to see who is hired to replace May. That coach will then have five days to convince the players to stay before the transfer window opens. If he can't, there will be a rush to land those that leave.

The summer months are usually fairly slow in terms of big news. This changes that. It's a major story that will have a dramatic effect on the college basketball world and, potentially, on the Kentucky Wildcats.

This article originally appeared on UK Wildcats Wire: Kentucky basketball potentially affected by Michigan coaching change

title: Senegal Trade Minister Says Government Open to Debt Revamp
description: Senegal is open to a debt restructuring as mounting payment pressures threaten to strain key sectors of the economy, according to a senior member of President Bassirou Diomaye Faye’s cabinet.
fullText: PoliticsBy Katarina Hoije and Jorgelina Do RosarioSaveSenegal is open to a debt restructuring as mounting payment pressures threaten to strain key sectors of the economy, according to a senior member of President Bassirou Diomaye Faye’s cabinet.Minister of Industry and Trade Serigne Gueye Diop said the government has been prioritizing debt servicing and has paid some external obligations ahead of schedule — including a eurobond earlier this month — but this approach was shifting.

title: I Tracked LLM Pricing for 8 Weeks. Here's What the Data Shows
description: Real pricing data from 1,111 AI models across 33 providers, tracked daily since April 24, 2026. The spread, the movers, and what it means for your infrastructure bill.
fullText:
A few months ago, I was building an AI product and hit a wall: Which LLM should we use?

The question sounds simple. But the pricing landscape for AI models shifts constantly and without warning. OpenAI has 69 models listed on their pricing page. Google has 18. Anthropic launched three new Claude versions in the span of weeks. By the time I had compared options and made a decision, I had no confidence the numbers were still current.

I checked pricing pages. I checked docs. I found no single place that told me:

So I automated it. Starting April 24, 2026, I built a daily scraper that pulls pricing data from every major provider. Here is what 8 weeks of data actually shows.

The headline finding is not dramatic: Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Mistral, and xAI all held their prices flat for the entire 59-day period. Not a single price change detected across those five providers.

That is useful information on its own. If you are budgeting around these providers, your cost model from April is still accurate today.

Here is the current pricing snapshot for the most-used models, accurate as of June 22:

The bigger story is not volatility. It is the 600x price range that now exists across the tracked catalog, from sub-cent inference to frontier flagship models.

Groq's Llama 3.1 8B costs $0.05 per million input tokens. GPT-4 Turbo costs $10.00 (200x). GPT-5.4 Pro costs $30.00 (600x). All three are in our dataset today. DeepSeek V4 Pro at $0.44 per million input tokens consistently ranks near the top on reasoning benchmarks at a fraction of the flagship price.

The question teams should be asking is not just “which model is best?” but “which model is best for this specific task given the cost?” For many production workloads, the answer is not the model you started with.

Over 59 days, our scraper detected 5 pricing changes across all 1,111 models. Every single one was on Together AI, a hosting aggregator that runs third-party open-source models.

The biggest move: Qwen37-max dropped 50% overnight on May 27, with no public announcement that reached mainstream channels. Teams running that model through Together AI saw their costs cut in half. Teams not monitoring pricing had no idea.

Three models saw price increases on June 2: Qwen3.5-9B (+70%), Llama-3.3-70B-Turbo (+18%), and Meta-Llama-3-8B-Lite (+40%). Not all pricing movement favors the buyer.

Across all 5 detected changes, the announcement path was the same: nothing sent directly to users. One change (DeepSeek-V4-Pro via Together) appeared in a provider blog post days later. The rest were silent.

Compare that to how other infrastructure pricing works:

LLM providers are operating more like spot markets than enterprise software. Prices move when they move. Most teams find out on their invoice.

After a few weeks of running this manually, I automated it and opened it up. Token Prices tracks 1,100+ models across 33 providers daily and surfaces changes the moment they happen.

The free tier covers the 10 major providers. Paid plans add historical depth, more providers, and API access. tokenprices.io

Let us know at support@tokenprices.io

title: I built an AI wearable demo that sees what I'm looking at and answers out loud
description: This Loom walks through making a western omelette, including how to check pan readiness and when to add ingredients. The presenter confirms there is a light coating of oil and tests the pan by sprinkling a drop of water to see it sizzle, noting it usually takes about a minute on medium heat. They pour in whisked eggs, let them cook until small bubbles form around the edges, and then add chopped veggies. Cheese should be sprinkled when the eggs are mostly set but still slightly running, then the omelette is folded and cooked briefly to finish. The video also notes the heat might be slightly high and advises lowering it to avoid overcooking.

Installing preflight-pro exposes the universal preflight command in your shell.

PreFlight is now powered by deeper local analysis primitives:

This is the core PreFlight signal. Every scan resolves into one of three clear outcomes so you know whether to stop, review, or ship.

PreFlight runs fixes in a strict sequence:

The first 10 patch applications are free across both phases. After that, a PREFLIGHT_PRO_KEY is required.

PreFlight can run directly in the terminal, through the VS Code/Cursor extension, or as an MCP server for AI-native editors.

scan_project remains free and unlimited. preflight_fix shares the global 10-patch free allowance before a PREFLIGHT_PRO_KEY is required.

PreFlight is designed to be used as a closed loop, not a one-shot scanner:
